codex_response:
Implemented `4852f2fa` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4852f2fa/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4852f2fa/verifier.py#L4), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4852f2fa/generator.py#L15), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4852f2fa/notes.md#L1). The rule is: crop the 8-cells to their tight box, pad to the smallest enclosing square on the top and/or left as needed, then repeat that square tile horizontally once per 4-cell. I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint as the starting hypothesis and documented that the Sonnet hint was incomplete because it missed the square-padding step.

The generator matches the task family by using only official-style 3x3 output tiles, deriving the input 8-object by removing any leading top/left padding, keeping the official 9x9 black canvas, and scattering 1-4 yellow singleton counters independently. No new primitive was added.

Verification passed end-to-end: official examples `7/7`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and the preview sheet looked on-distribution against the originals.
